enums.name: Origin

values.name: user
values.doc: Indicates the operation is initiated by a user.

values.name: system
values.doc: Indicates the operation is initiated by a system.

values.name: user,system
values.doc: Indicates the operation is initiated by a user or system.

enums.doc: The intended executor of the operation; as in Resource Based Access Control (RBAC) and audit logs UX. Default value is "user,system"

enums.name: ActionType
enums.doc: Extensible enum. Indicates the action type. "Internal" refers to actions that are for internal only APIs.

enums.name: ScheduledActionDeadlineType

values.name: InitiateAt
values.doc: Initiate the operation at the given deadline.

values.name: CompleteBy
values.doc: Complete the operation by the given deadline.

enums.doc: The types of deadlines supported by ScheduledActions

enums.name: ScheduledActionOptimizationPreference

values.name: Availability
values.doc: Optimize while considering availability of resources

values.name: CostAvailabilityBalanced
values.doc: Optimize while considering a balance of cost and availability

enums.doc: The preferences customers can select to optimize their requests to ScheduledActions

enums.name: ResourceOperationType

values.name: Unknown
values.doc: The default value for this enum type

enums.doc: The kind of operation types that can be performed on resources using ScheduledActions

enums.name: ScheduledActionOperationState

values.name: Unknown
values.doc: The default value for the operation state enum

values.name: PendingExecution
values.doc: Operations that are waiting to be executed

values.name: Executing
values.doc: Operations that are in the process of being executed

values.name: Cancelled
values.doc: Operations that have been Cancelled by the user

enums.doc: Values that define the states of operations in Scheduled Actions

enums.name: ScheduledActionResourceType

values.name: VirtualMachineScaleSet
values.doc: Resources defined are Virtual Machines Scale Sets

enums.doc: The type of resource being targeted

enums.name: ScheduledActionType

values.name: Start
values.doc: Perform a start action on the specified resources

values.name: Deallocate
values.doc: Perform a deallocate action on the specified resources

values.name: Hibernate
values.doc: Perform hibernate and deallocate on the specified resources

enums.doc: Specify which action user wants to be performed on the resources

enums.name: ScheduledActionsScheduleWeekDay
enums.doc: Representation of the possible selection of days in a week in a gregorian calendar

enums.name: ScheduledActionsScheduleMonth
enums.doc: Representation of the months available selection in a gregorian calendar

enums.name: createdByType

values.name: User
values.doc: The entity was created by a user.

values.name: Application
values.doc: The entity was created by an application.

values.name: ManagedIdentity
values.doc: The entity was created by a managed identity.

values.name: Key
values.doc: The entity was created by a key.

enums.doc: The kind of entity that created the resource.

enums.name: OccurrenceResourceProvisioningState
enums.doc: The provisioning state of a resource type.

enums.name: ScheduledActionResourceOperationStatus
enums.doc: The state the resource is on after the resource operation is applied

enums.name: ScheduledActionOccurrenceState

values.name: Failed
values.doc: The occurrence has failed during its scheduling

values.name: Cancelling
values.doc: The occurrence is going through cancellation

enums.doc: The state the occurrence is at a given time

models.name: OperationListResult
models.doc: A list of REST API operations supported by an Azure Resource Provider. It contains an URL link to get the next set of results.

properties.name: value
properties.doc: The Operation items on this page

valueType.name: Operation
valueType.doc: Details of a REST API operation, returned from the Resource Provider Operations API

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the operation, as per Resource-Based Access Control (RBAC). Examples: "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/write", "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/capture/action"

properties.name: isDataAction
properties.doc: Whether the operation applies to data-plane. This is "true" for data-plane operations and "false" for Azure Resource Manager/control-plane operations.

properties.name: display
properties.doc: Localized display information for this particular operation.

type.name: OperationDisplay
type.doc: Localized display information for an operation.

properties.name: provider
properties.doc: The localized friendly form of the resource provider name, e.g. "Microsoft Monitoring Insights" or "Microsoft Compute".

properties.name: resource
properties.doc: The localized friendly name of the resource type related to this operation. E.g. "Virtual Machines" or "Job Schedule Collections".

properties.name: operation
properties.doc: The concise, localized friendly name for the operation; suitable for dropdowns. E.g. "Create or Update Virtual Machine", "Restart Virtual Machine".

properties.name: description
properties.doc: The short, localized friendly description of the operation; suitable for tool tips and detailed views.

properties.name: origin
properties.doc: The intended executor of the operation; as in Resource Based Access Control (RBAC) and audit logs UX. Default value is "user,system"

properties.name: actionType
properties.doc: Extensible enum. Indicates the action type. "Internal" refers to actions that are for internal only APIs.

properties.name: nextLink
properties.doc: The link to the next page of items

properties.name: resourceConfigParameters
properties.doc: resource creation payload

type.name: ResourceProvisionPayload
type.doc: Resource creation data model

properties.name: baseProfile
properties.doc: JSON object that contains VM properties that are common across all VMs in this batch (if you want to create 100 VMs in this request, and they all have same vmSize, then include vmSize in baseProfile)

properties.name: resourceOverrides
properties.doc: JSON array, that contains VM properties that should to be overridden for each VM in the batch (if you want to create 100 VMs, they all need a distinct computerName property, you pass computerNames for each VM in batch in this array), service will merge baseProfile with VM specific overrides and create a merged VMProfile.

properties.name: resourceCount
properties.doc: Number of VMs to be created

properties.name: resourcePrefix
properties.doc: if resourceOverrides doesn't contain "name", service will create name based of prefix and ResourceCount e.g. resourceprefix-0,resourceprefix-1..

properties.name: id
properties.doc: Fully qualified resource ID for the resource. Ex - /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/{resourceProviderNamespace}/{resourceType}/{resourceName}

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the resource

properties.name: type
properties.doc: The type of the resource. E.g. "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines" or "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts"

properties.name: systemData
properties.doc: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

type.name: SystemData
type.doc: Metadata pertaining to creation and last modification of the resource.

properties.name: createdBy
properties.doc: The identity that created the resource.

properties.name: createdByType
properties.doc: The type of identity that created the resource.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.doc: The timestamp of resource creation (UTC).

properties.name: lastModifiedBy
properties.doc: The identity that last modified the resource.

properties.name: lastModifiedByType
properties.doc: The type of identity that last modified the resource.

properties.name: lastModifiedAt
properties.doc: The timestamp of resource last modification (UTC)

properties.doc: The geo-location where the resource lives

properties.name: resourceType
properties.doc: The type of resource the scheduled action is targeting

properties.doc: The action the scheduled action should perform in the resources

properties.name: startTime
properties.doc: The time which the scheduled action is supposed to start running

properties.name: endTime
properties.doc: The time when the scheduled action is supposed to stop scheduling

properties.name: schedule
properties.doc: The schedule the scheduled action is supposed to follow

properties.name: scheduledTime
properties.doc: The time the scheduled action is supposed to run on

properties.name: timeZone
properties.doc: The timezone the scheduled time is specified on

properties.name: requestedWeekDays
properties.doc: The week days the scheduled action is supposed to run on

properties.name: requestedMonths
properties.doc: The months the scheduled action is supposed to run on

properties.name: requestedDaysOfTheMonth
properties.doc: The days of the month the scheduled action is supposed to run on. If empty, it means it will run on every day of the month.

properties.name: executionParameters
properties.doc: The execution parameters the scheduled action is supposed to follow

properties.name: deadlineType
properties.doc: The type of deadline the scheduled action is supposed to follow for the schedule. If no value is passed, it will default to InitiateAt.

properties.name: notificationSettings
properties.doc: The notification settings for the scheduled action

valueType.doc: The information about notifications to be send to about upcoming operations.

properties.doc: Where the notification should be sent. For email, it should follow email format.

properties.doc: Type of notification to be sent.

properties.name: language
properties.doc: The language the notification should be sent on.

properties.name: IsDisabled
properties.doc: Tells if the notification is enabled or not.

properties.name: disabled
properties.doc: Tell if the scheduled action is disabled or not

properties.doc: The status of the last provisioning operation performed on the resource.
